stories.title: Bugs and logic errors
stories.description: Find logic errors, incorrect conditions, wrong return values, off-by-one errors, and code that doesn't do what its name promises.
acceptanceCriteria: No function returns wrong results for valid inputs
No condition is inverted or missing a case
Error paths are reachable and behave correctly
Edge cases (empty input, null, boundary values) are handled

stories.title: Error handling completeness
stories.description: Every file I/O, process spawn, and external call must handle failures. No operation should crash the process with an unhandled exception.
acceptanceCriteria: All readFileSync/writeFileSync/renameSync/unlinkSync in try-catch
All execSync/spawn calls handle failures gracefully
Error messages include operation context (what failed and where)
Best-effort operations are documented (won't crash on failure)

stories.title: Test coverage and quality
stories.description: Every exported function needs tests. Critical paths (convergence, round execution, parallel groups, claim release, state flush) need edge case coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: Every exported function in src/ has at least one test
Convergent loop behavior tested: re-evaluation, regression detection, convergence exit, no_progress exit
Parallel group execution tested: stories in same group run concurrently
Edge cases: empty PRD, corrupt JSON, missing files, concurrent access

stories.title: Type safety
stories.description: No unnecessary any types, no unsafe type assertions. Zod schemas match TypeScript interfaces. All config fields are actually used.
acceptanceCriteria: tsc --noEmit passes clean
No 'any' or 'as any' in src/ (tests are ok)
Every RalphConfig field is read somewhere in the codebase
Zod schema defaults match what the code assumes

stories.title: Documentation accuracy
stories.description: CLAUDE.md, DESIGN.md, and PLAN.md must match the actual codebase. File paths, CLI commands, architecture descriptions, and type definitions must be accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md source tree matches actual files on disk
All CLI commands in docs exist in src/cli/index.ts with matching flags
Architecture descriptions match code (convergent model, group parallelism, two loop modes)
No references to removed or renamed features

stories.title: Dead code and unused exports
stories.description: No unused imports, no orphaned files, no exported functions that nothing imports. Every module should be reachable from the CLI entry point or tests.
acceptanceCriteria: No unused imports in any src/ file
No exported function that is never imported (in src/ or tests/)
No files in src/ that are never imported by anything
npm test passes after any removals

stories.title: Interface and type consistency
stories.description: Types should match their usage. Interfaces should be fully implemented. The same concept should not be represented differently in different places.
acceptanceCriteria: IAgent interface is fully implemented by ClaudeCodeAgent
All RunState fields are populated by StateWriter at appropriate times
IterationRecord fields match what the loop actually records
Story type matches what loadPrd returns and what the loop consumes
ExitReason values are all reachable from the loop

stories.title: Unnecessary complexity
stories.description: Find code that is more complex than needed. Duplicate logic, abstractions wrapping one implementation, config fields with no effect, features that could be removed.
acceptanceCriteria: No duplicate logic across modules
No wrapper abstractions that add no value
No config fields that are never read
No utility functions used only once that could be inlined

stories.title: Convergence methodology soundness
stories.description: Verify the convergent loop actually converges correctly. Check that: group parallelism works, re-evaluation catches regressions, backlog vs convergent modes behave differently, per-story CB works, maxRounds limits rounds not iterations.
acceptanceCriteria: Convergent mode re-runs all stories each round (not just failing ones)
Backlog mode only runs failing stories
Group parallelism: stories in same group run via Promise.all
Re-evaluation at round start flips regressions back to passes=false
maxRounds limits rounds, maxIterations limits total agent calls
Per-story consecutiveFailures skips stuck stories
The loop can actually reach 'converged' exit (not just max_iterations)

stories.title: Build, packaging, and runtime
stories.description: The project builds, the CLI works, dependencies are correct, .gitignore is complete. The tool can actually be invoked end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run build succeeds
dist/cli/index.js has shebang and is executable
package.json bin/main entries are correct
No unnecessary dependencies
.gitignore covers node_modules, dist, .ralph/runs, tsbuildinfo

stories.title: Security and safety guardrails
stories.description: Protected paths are enforced. Budget limits actually stop the loop. The circuit breaker actually trips. No way to bypass safety mechanisms.
acceptanceCriteria: Protected path validation cannot be bypassed by the agent
maxCostUsd and maxTokensSession actually stop the loop mid-round
Circuit breaker trips after configured threshold and is checked between stories
No file operations outside the project directory
Claims file locking prevents race conditions

stories.title: Crash recovery and state durability
stories.description: If the process crashes, state should be recoverable. Atomic writes should prevent corruption. Stale claims should be cleaned up. Resume should work.
acceptanceCriteria: State writer uses atomic temp+rename for all writes
Stale claims (dead PID, expired heartbeat) are pruned on next access
resume correctly finds interrupted/paused runs
Signal handler registers for SIGINT/SIGTERM and triggers graceful shutdown
Best-effort status update on crash path
